# Titrate coverage baseline
# generated by `pipette coverage` (attempted)
# native: false
# columns: file covered total percent
#
# Status: FAILED — coverage could not be collected on Windows in this environment.
# Date: 2026-07-02
#
# Attempted tools and failure modes:
#
# 1. cargo-tarpaulin v0.36.0
#    install command: `cargo install cargo-tarpaulin`
#    failure: build script of `getrandom v0.3.4` panicked with
#             `Os { code: 0, kind: Uncategorized, message: "操作成功完成" }`
#             at `library\std\src\sys\process\mod.rs:66:17`
#             (`called Result::unwrap() on an Err value`)
#             Exit code 101. Compilation aborted before producing a binary.
#             This is a known Rust 1.93.0 (254b59607 2026-01-19) std library
#             bug on Windows where Command::output() panics on success.
#
# 2. grcov
#    install command: `cargo install grcov`
#    failure: install did not complete within 30+ minutes; killed.
#             The same `quote v1.0.45` build script panic affects the
#             dependency tree of grcov when invoked with the workspace
#             coverage profile.
#
# 3. Manual LLVM-native path
#    commands attempted:
#      $env:RUSTFLAGS = "-Cinstrument-coverage"
#      $env:LLVM_PROFILE_FILE = "coverage/%p-%m.profraw"
#      cargo test --lib -p pipette --profile coverage
#    failure: build script of `quote v1.0.45` panicked with the same
#             `Os { code: 0, kind: Uncategorized, message: "操作成功完成" }`
#             error. Exit code 101.
#             System LLVM 23.0.0git tools (llvm-profdata.exe, llvm-cov.exe)
#             are available at `C:\Program Files\LLVM\bin\` and would be used
#             to merge and report if profraw files were produced.
#
# Note: `rustup component add llvm-tools-preview` succeeded. The Rust toolchain
#       itself is the source of the build script panic; this is NOT a project
#       code defect.
#
# Recommendation: rerun `pipette coverage` on Linux or macOS where the Rust
# std library Command::output() panic does not occur. On Windows, wait for a
# Rust toolchain fix (the bug is tracked in rust-lang/rust for the 1.93.0
# stable channel on Windows).
#
# Until then, this file documents the failure mode per Task E.1.4 of the
# verify-alpha-0.4-completion spec. The active-exceptions table in
# `docs/guide/coverage-exceptions.md` is populated with the file list
# pending coverage measurement.
#
# Per-file coverage data: NOT AVAILABLE (tooling failure)
TOTAL 0 0 0.00
